sourceTitle: Notion for engineers: A comprehensive guide to streamlining project management

questions.question: Where can engineers quickly find pre‑made Notion templates for setting up a workspace?

options.text: By clicking the Templates button in the sidebar
options.explanation: The article states that engineers can click the Templates button in the sidebar to access pre‑made templates.

options.text: In the Settings > Integrations menu
options.explanation: Templates are not located under Settings; they are accessed via the Templates button or the Template Gallery.

options.text: Through the Notion mobile app only
options.explanation: Templates are available on both desktop and web versions, not exclusively on mobile.

options.text: By searching the Notion community forums
options.explanation: While community forums may have templates, the article points to the built‑in Templates button and Template Gallery.

questions.question: What is a unique characteristic of each entry in a Notion Tasks database?

options.text: It is also a full Notion page that can hold rich content
options.explanation: The article explains that every database entry is a page where you can add text, code snippets, and embedded content.

options.text: It can only contain plain text and a due‑date
options.explanation: Entries can contain much more than plain text; they can hold rich media and various properties.

options.text: It is limited to a single line description
options.explanation: Notion pages are not limited to a single line; they support extensive content.

options.text: It cannot be linked to other pages
options.explanation: Pages can be linked, embedded, or referenced elsewhere in Notion.

questions.question: Which view types are mentioned for displaying tasks in Notion?

options.text: Table and List
options.explanation: The article specifically says you can view tasks in a table or a list.

options.text: Calendar and Board
options.explanation: Calendar and Board are valid Notion views but they are not the ones called out in this guide.

options.text: Gallery and Timeline
options.explanation: These are also Notion view options, yet they are not mentioned in the article.

options.text: Kanban and Gantt
options.explanation: Kanban (Board) and Gantt (Timeline) exist, but the guide only references Table and List.

questions.question: How does Notion enable engineers to organize documentation hierarchically?

options.text: By nesting pages inside pages indefinitely, creating subpages
options.explanation: The guide highlights that you can nest pages within pages without limit, forming a hierarchical wiki.

options.text: By using a fixed three‑level folder structure
options.explanation: Notion does not impose a fixed depth; nesting is unlimited.

options.text: By tagging pages with hierarchical labels
options.explanation: Tags can be used, but hierarchy is achieved through page nesting, not labeling.

options.text: By creating separate databases for each documentation level
options.explanation: While databases can organize data, the article emphasizes page nesting for hierarchical documentation.

questions.question: Which properties can be added to a Tasks database to help track engineering workflows?

options.text: Assignee, status, priority, and due date
options.explanation: The article lists these four properties as examples you can add to a Tasks database.

options.text: File size, pixel density, and frame rate
options.explanation: These properties relate to media assets, not to task tracking.

options.text: Operating system, CPU architecture, and memory usage
options.explanation: These are system specs, not the task‑specific properties highlighted in the guide.

options.text: Author, publication date, and ISBN
options.explanation: Those properties belong to bibliographic entries, not engineering tasks.

questions.question: What advantage does embedding content (e.g., Figma designs or Loom videos) inside a task page provide?

options.text: All relevant information stays in one place, reducing context switching
options.explanation: Embedding keeps design files, videos, and notes together with the task, streamlining workflow.

options.text: It automatically converts the embedded file into plain text
options.explanation: Embedding preserves the original format; Notion does not convert it to plain text.

options.text: It disables version control for the embedded assets
options.explanation: Embedding does not affect version control of the original files.

options.text: It forces the task to be displayed only in a List view
options.explanation: Embedding does not restrict the view type; tasks can still be shown in Table, List, etc.

questions.question: If an engineering team wants Notion to serve as a quarter‑by‑quarter roadmap, which feature is most essential?

options.text: Customizable databases with date‑based properties
options.explanation: Roadmaps rely on time‑based data; databases with date properties allow planning across quarters.

options.text: The ability to embed PDF manuals
options.explanation: While useful, PDFs don’t provide the timeline structure needed for a roadmap.

options.text: Unlimited page nesting depth
options.explanation: Depth helps with documentation hierarchy, but a roadmap primarily needs date tracking.

options.text: Integration with external email clients
options.explanation: Email integration is unrelated to visualizing a quarterly roadmap.

questions.question: Why might an engineer choose to use Notion as a "team wiki" rather than a dedicated documentation tool?

options.text: Because Notion lets you nest pages infinitely, enabling a highly flexible knowledge base within a single workspace
options.explanation: Infinite nesting provides the flexibility to structure documentation in any hierarchy, a key advantage highlighted in the guide.

options.text: Because Notion automatically generates API documentation from code comments
options.explanation: Notion does not generate API docs; this feature belongs to specialized documentation generators.

options.text: Because Notion enforces a strict version‑control workflow similar to Git
options.explanation: Notion offers version history but not a Git‑style branching system.

options.text: Because Notion limits the size of each page, ensuring concise documentation
options.explanation: Notion does not impose strict size limits; pages can be as large as needed.

questions.question: What is a potential drawback of relying solely on Notion's built‑in task views (table or list) for large engineering projects?

options.text: Complex filtering and sorting may become cumbersome without custom formulas or third‑party integrations
options.explanation: While tables and lists are powerful, scaling to large datasets often requires advanced filters or external tools, which can be a limitation.

options.text: Tasks cannot be assigned to multiple users at once
options.explanation: Notion allows multi‑select assignee properties; the limitation is not about assignment.

options.text: Embedded videos automatically download, consuming bandwidth
options.explanation: Embedding streams content; it does not trigger automatic downloads.

options.text: Table view disables the ability to add code snippets to tasks
options.explanation: Code snippets can be added within the page of each task, regardless of view.

questions.question: According to the guide, what is the recommended first step for an engineer who wants to start using Notion for project management?

options.text: Browse the template gallery and select a suitable template
options.explanation: The guide suggests starting with a template from the gallery to set up the workspace quickly.

options.text: Write a custom API integration from scratch
options.explanation: Custom API work is not presented as the first step; templates are the entry point.

options.text: Create a new workspace for every engineering team member
options.explanation: Creating many workspaces is unnecessary; the guide recommends using a single workspace with templates.

options.text: Install the Notion mobile app before the desktop version
options.explanation: The guide does not prioritize mobile installation; it focuses on template selection.
